data.title: Member of board overseeing railroad mergers, Robert E. Primus, ousted by White House. He plans to carry on despite termination.
data.description: Robert E. Primus was informed via email from the White House that his position had been terminated. Despite this, he has decided to carry on with his responsibilities.

data.title: Southern restaurant Full Moon Bar-B-Que donates $200,000 worth of backpacks & school supplies, benefiting 900 children in need.
data.description: In Birmingham, Alabama, Full Moon Bar-B-Que's 'BACKPACK BLESSINGS' program has provided 900 children with essential supplies. The Southern barbecue brand has exceeded $200,000 in total donations for this initiative, which has been running for six years.

data.title: Sharp decline in copper-to-gold ratio to 0.0015, lowest since March 2020, signals global economic growth fears and loss of investor confidence.
data.description: The copper-to-gold ratio, an important economic indicator, has dropped to 0.0015, the lowest level seen since March 2020, indicating global growth fears. This ratio compares the value of copper to gold, with copper being a measure of economic expansion and gold reflecting investor safety during downturns. A decline in this ratio suggests a lack of confidence in economic recovery, with a low ratio signaling a "risk-off" sentiment. The significance of this drop hinting at possible major slowdowns ahead can be deduced from this ratio. This development is an indicator of concerns regarding the overall economic scenario.

data.title: Urban Outfitters beats Q2 expectations with $1.58 EPS and $1.5B revenue. Shares drop 8% post-earnings. CEO notes record profits for the quarter.
data.description: Urban Outfitters Inc. (NASDAQ: URBN) reported impressive second-quarter results after the market closed on Wednesday. The company posted earnings of $1.58 per share, surpassing the expected $1.48 per share. Revenue for the quarter stood at $1.5 billion, also surpassing the anticipated $1.47 billion. CEO Richard A. Hayne expressed pride in achieving record revenues, profits, and earnings per share, highlighting positive comparable sales across all five brands in various regions. Following the announcement, Urban Outfitters shares saw an 8% decline, settling at $71.77 on Thursday. For further details, refer to the full article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: James Altucher discusses Elon Musk's latest AI breakthrough in a presentation, which could boost US innovation.
data.description: A recent presentation by James Altucher reveals the potential impact of Musk's latest AI breakthrough on U.S. innovation and the technology landscape. Altucher discusses how this advancement could speed up innovation in the U.S. and reshape the future of technology, bringing about significant transformations.

data.title: Teva launches generic GLP-1 weight loss drug with FDA approval for Saxenda, providing first-ever generic option.
data.description: The pharmaceutical industry is amplifying its focus on developing generic drugs to meet the rising demand for affordable healthcare options. This has resulted in major market players pursuing FDA approvals for generic versions of popular medications, leading to a shift in the competitive landscape and offering more options for consumers. Recently, Teva Pharmaceuticals, Inc., a U.S. subsidiary of Teva Pharmaceutical Industries Ltd., disclosed the FDA approval and subsequent launch of a generic iteration of Novo Nordisk A/S' Saxenda (liraglutide injection). This move marks a significant milestone as it introduces the first generic GLP-1 product specifically indicated for weight loss in the U.S. This development signifies a step towards enhanced accessibility and reduced costs in the healthcare sector.

data.title: Casella Waste Systems announces pricing of $37.5 million in remarketed Bonds with a new interest rate of 4.250%, effective September 2, 2025.
data.description: Casella Waste Systems, Inc. has announced the remarketing of $37.5 million aggregate principal amount of New York State Environmental Facilities Corporation Solid Waste Disposal Revenue Bonds. Originally issued at $40 million, the bonds have a final maturity of September 1, 2050. Casella will redeem $2.5 million on September 2, 2025, with the remaining $37.5 million to be remarketed at a new interest rate of 4.250% per annum. The interest rate period under the previous issuance expires on September 1, 2025, making the bonds subject to mandatory tender on September 2, 2025. The remarketing and redemption process is expected to be completed on September 2, 2025.
